item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
accidentally reverted page back to an earlier version due to a misclick, fixed it, sorry!

this is really great, I'm surprised I haven't read this before. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Their quote is hilarious, +1.
Do you think they got the memo that the food pyramid changed?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This similarity between the effects of SCP-1630, and SCP-643 are noteworthy, both being food objects that cause compulsive eating effects. It wouldn't be to far out of line for a link of to SCP-643 to be thrown in here. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

On June 2, 2010, a new instance of SCP-1630 was contained that appeared to be composed of various sugars, fats, and artificial substances. Classified as SCP-1630-26, it was interred in a normal containment chamber. Following initial containment, SCP-1630-26 was tested by participating in a Delta Event performed in a controlled environment. During the event, attending personnel reported nausea and sweet tastes in their mouth. Various documents within Site-77 were replaced with advertisements for various fast food and confectionery corporations. Following the end of the Delta Event, SCP-1630-26 was moved off-site, and containment procedures have been slated for minor updates.

To this:

On June 2, 2010, a new instance of SCP-1630 was contained that appeared to be composed of various sugars, fats, and artificial substances. Classified as SCP-1630-26, it was interred in a normal containment chamber. Following initial containment, SCP-1630-26 was tested by participating in a Delta Event performed in a controlled environment. During the event, attending personnel reported nausea and sweet tastes in their mouth. Various documents within Site-77 were replaced with advertisements for various fast food and confectionery corporations1. Following the end of the Delta Event, SCP-1630-26 was moved off-site, and containment procedures have been slated for minor updates.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. It was noted that documentation pertaining to SCP-643 remained unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was okay with it until the pyramid started turning everything into food… Completely ruined it..

item.title: Late post
item.content:encoded: Does anyone else think this is connected somehow to 1690?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: mirroring azzleflux two years ago, this may be the first time I've thought "What the hell did I just read" after finishing a skip and LIKED it.+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess you won't be upvoting, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll upvote when I don't know what show the picture came from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the picture supposed to be the cottage cheese dude?

Because the caption say -7, but cottage cheese man is -22.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There have been no claims made saying that this image is owned or intellectual property of the author, and it's not like he's using it for personal gain or anything, so…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, so, can someone from the staff delete that picture?

item.title: Admin Post
item.content:encoded: It's not your place to remove someone elses picture. If you feel that someone is plagiarizing, bring it up to staff, but do NOT make edits yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I removed the picture, it's plagiarized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw the picture, and I upvoted.

I read the article, and I wanted to upvote again. Delightfully bizarre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's an intriguing question…
Would a vegan be able to eat a steamed slice of Swamp Thing; or Audrey 2 from "Little Shop of Horrors"?
For my money, the ethics of veganism hinge on wether or not the proposed food can experience suffering. Bean plants can't, but bean-men can; ergo the Food Group-ies would be right out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather inventive. I'm curious if vegans could eat the humanoids made of garlic and beans. On one hand they're plants which vegans can eat but their apparently sentient creatures which vegans won't eat…are their any vegan D-Class personnel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be the first time I've thought "What the hell did I just read" after finishing a skip and not liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I second the comment on the Delta Event. I don't think it's ever explicitly stated what a Delta Event is in the article.

Also, why isn't 1630-26 included in the description? I know it said the containment procedures needed to be revised, but the description is part of the overall documentation and not the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MOMMY, WHY IS THE COTTAGE CHEESE MAN EATING HIMSELF?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said earlier, the inspiration came from seeing the guy and thinking "what if he was made of cottage cheese."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really feel anything with this one, so no vote for now. I just can't get a good grasp of the implications of this phenomenon. Are people getting actually fed or just feel contended? Is the matter turned into food permanently and is it actually edible? Couldn't we solve world hunger with something like this? However I really like the addendum.

Just curious: Was the general idea also inspired by the meat-monster in John dies at the end?

average intelligence for human subjects between the ages of 20 and 26

Why such a very specific age bracket and not just ''adults''?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed that "termination" is being misused in this context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You don't edit posts by replying. You go to options->edit.

And thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edit: Also, the article's pretty good too :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is. Maybe the picture is a bit recognizable, but I'm sure it wouldn't be difficult to scrounge up something similar yet less often seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, why should he black the date out? We don't just redact things just because.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tone seems pretty off for me, then again I feel like I emphasize tone more than most. If you want I can provide specifics.

Also,

As of 10/03/1981, SCP-1630 has been classified as Euclid.

Date should be blacked out.

And the explanation of a Delta Event doesn't state that what's occuring is a Delta Event until pretty late.

Overall I like it a whole lot, it's fun and creative (and even earned my upvote), but it needs some serious refining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess "unbelievable" is the wrong term in this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved to read it. when you said the idea in the chat i knew you where going to make something awesome out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like Dr. Wondertainment was commissioned to do a public-service announcement, and decided to do it via a bunch of Little Misters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From the food pyramid bit on, it sort of becomes unbelievable for me. People made of food? Okay. People made of food that climb on top of each other, and then people around them are suddenly fed, and everything becomes food? Ehhhhh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't watch that show, so I wouldn't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't the image The Watcher from Doctor Who?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article came to be because I though "what if that guy was white because he was made of cottage cheese" followed by "food people" and finally "human food pyramid. Then I posted the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've had my eye on that fuel image for a while, but you used it in a much better way than I'd been planning. Well played, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that. I'll see about doing an edit before I go to Tampa tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun and weird, but the containment breach allowing a Delta Event to occur felt contrived. It would be more believable to me if they thought "Hey, this is the guy that the food pyramid says we shouldn't eat, wonder what would happen if we put it together with a few of the others" and then let it happen as an experiment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's ok. As a whole, you did a great job (specially with the cheese guy that eats itself… But I think I saw that before). Now, I seriously dislike the self-termination term. You can use "its own expiration" or "accidental suicide", but self-termination sounds like if the Foundation ordered it to eat itself. No vote.

P.D: Several non redundant sentences look so redundant D: (even this sad face).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the implications of the obesity instance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's delightfully weird, like I'm watching some Japanese infomercial. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was fun and inventive in the chat, and it's fun and inventive now.
